results.title: Latvia's measures to counter money laundering, terrorist financing and proliferation financing
results.description: This report assesses the effectiveness of Latvia’s measures against money laundering, terrorist financing and proliferation financing and their level of compliance with the Financial Action Task Force Recommendations, at the time of the on-site visit in November 2024.

results.title: Jurisdictions under Increased Monitoring - 13 February 2026
results.description:
Jurisdictions under increased monitoring are actively working with the FATF to address strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing.
The FATF now also identifies Kuwait and Papua New Guinea.

results.title: High-Risk Jurisdictions subject to a Call for Action - 13 February 2026
results.description: High-risk jurisdictions have significant strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and financing of proliferation. For all countries identified as high-risk, the FATF calls on all members and urges all jurisdictions to apply enhanced due diligence, and, in the most serious cases, countries are called upon to apply countermeasures to protect the international financial system from the money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing (ML/TF/PF) risks emanating from the country.

results.title: Outcomes FATF Plenary, 11-13 February 2026
results.description: The fifth Financial Action Task Force (FATF) Plenary meeting under the Mexican Presidency of Elisa de Anda Madrazo concluded today, with action agreed to help stop fraudsters and other criminals from profiting from illicit activity.

results.title: FATF Week in Mexico City - February 2026
results.description: The fifth Financial Action Task Force (FATF) Plenary under the two-year Mexican Presidency of Elisa de Anda Madrazo will take place in Mexico City on 11¬–13 February, concluding FATF Week, which starts on 9 February.

results.title: British Virgin Islands' progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: British Virgin Islands has made some progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in its 2024 Mutual Evaluation.

results.title: Suriname's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: Suriname has made significant progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in its 2023 Mutual Evaluation.

results.title: Consolidated assessment ratings (2014 - 2025 assessments)
results.description: An overview of the ratings on both effectiveness and technical compliance for all countries assessed against the 2012 FATF Recommendations and using the 2013 Assessment Methodology.

results.title: Maldives' measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description:
This evaluation was based on the 2012 FATF Recommendations, and was prepared using the 2013 Methodology. The evaluation was based on information provided by the country, and information obtained by the evaluation team during its on-site visit to the country.

The findings of this assessment have been reviewed and endorsed by the FATF.

Please refer to the Executive Summary of the report for the Key Findings and Priority Actions.

results.title: Webinar: Exiting the FATF Grey List – Success Stories and Pathways to Sustainable Compliance
results.description: This joint African Development Bank and Financial Action Task Force webinar discussed key insights from the FATF grey list process. A recording is available in English and French.

results.title: Niue's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description:
This evaluation was based on the 2012 FATF Recommendations, and was prepared using the 2013 Methodology. The evaluation was based on information provided by the country, and information obtained by the evaluation team during its on-site visit to the country.

The findings of this assessment have been reviewed and endorsed by the FATF.

Please refer to the Executive Summary of the report for the Key Findings and Priority Actions.

results.title: Belgium's measures to counter money laundering, terrorist financing and proliferation financing
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force mutual evaluation of Belgium assessed the effectiveness of the country’s measures against money laundering, countering terrorist financing and proliferation financing (AML/CFT/CPF), and their level of compliance with the FATF Recommendations, at the time of an on-site visit between January-February 2025.

results.title: Malaysia's measures to counter money laundering, terrorist financing and proliferation financing
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force and Asia/Pacific Group on Money Laundering (APG) mutual evaluation of Malaysia assessed the effectiveness of the country’s anti-money laundering, countering terrorist financing and proliferation financing (AML/CFT/CPF) measures, and their level of compliance with the FATF Recommendations, at the time of an on-site visit in February 2025.

results.title: 2022 Procedures for the FATF AML/CFT/CPF Mutual Evaluations, Follow-Up and ICRG
results.description: This document sets out the Procedures for the mutual evaluations, follow-up and ICRG processes conducted during the 5th Round of Mutual Evaluations, against the FATF Methodology 2022. Updated in June 2025.

results.title: Consolidated Processes and Procedures for Mutual Evaluations and Follow-Up:  “Universal Procedures”
results.description: The set of core elements that apply to all anti-money laundering, counter-terrorist financing  and counter-proliferation financing assessments and follow-up processes, in accordance with the 2022 FATF Methodology. These Universal Procedures were last revised in October 2025.

results.title: Joint African Development Bank - Financial Action Task Force webinar: Exiting the FATF Grey List – Success Stories and Pathways to Sustainable Compliance
results.description: This month the African Development Bank and the Financial Action Task Force will bring together a panel of international experts to discuss key insights from the FATF grey list process. During an online webinar, speakers from countries that have recently exited the grey list will share knowledge and lessons learnt with public and private sectors practitioners around the world, aiming to support other countries to strengthen their defences against illicit finance.

results.title: Webinar: Promoting Financial Inclusion through a Risk-based Approach to AML/CFT
results.description: An international panel of experts discussed the recently strengthened FATF Standards on the Risk-Based Approach (Recommendation 1) and new Guidance on Financial Inclusion, and shared case studies and best practices from around the world.

results.description: This follow-up report analyses the progress of Myanmar in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in its Mutual Evaluation and Follow-Up Reports.

results.description: This report analyses Vietnam's progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in the 2022 assessment of their measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing.

results.title: 2013 Procedures for the FATF Fourth Round of AML/CFT Mutual Evaluations
results.description: This document sets out the procedures that are the basis for the fourth round of mutual evaluations which involves two inter-related components for technical compliance and effectiveness. Adopted in 2013, these Procedures were last amended in June 2025.

results.title: FATF and GIABA hold Joint Experts Meeting in Ghana, Accra
results.description: This week, the FATF and GIABA hosted the Joint Experts Meeting (JEM) in Accra, Ghana. Over the course of three days, more than 270 experts - including 73 from the private sector - from over 40 countries gathered to share the latest insights on money laundering and terrorist financing risks and responses.

results.title: FATF releases detailed guidance to help practitioners recover criminal assets
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) has today published new guidance and best practices that aim to intensify global efforts to recover criminal assets.

results.title: FATF to host webinar on Promoting Financial Inclusion through Risk-based Approach to AML/CFT
results.description: Next month, the FATF will hold a webinar on financial inclusion to strengthen countries’ understanding of how to adopt proportionate and risk-based approaches to tackling illicit finance.

results.title: 3rd Enhanced Follow-up report of Venezuela
results.description: This follow-up report analyses progress made by Venezuela in addressing certain technical compliance deficiencies identified in its Mutual Evaluation.

results.title: Update: Re-application of UN Security Council Resolutions related to Iran
results.description: Information relating to FATF Recommendation 7 which is applicable to all current Security Council resolutions applying targeted financial sanctions relating to the prevention and disruption of the financing of proliferation of weapons of mass destruction.

results.title: FATF President Speech at UN Security Council Briefing, August 2025
results.description:
Speech delivered by FATF President, Elisa de Anda Madrazo at the UN Security Council regarding the threats to international peace and security caused by terrorist acts - Security Council, 9982nd meeting.  

Twenty-first report of the Secretary-General on the threat posed by ISIL (Da'esh) to international peace and security and the range of United Nations efforts in support of Member States in countering the threat (S/2025/496)

results.title: FATF Code of Conduct
results.description:
The FATF is committed to fostering a respectful, inclusive and safe working environment for its members, stakeholders and staff and ensuring the organisation’s work is carried out with integrity and professionalism. 
The purpose of this Code of Conduct is to communicate what kind of conduct is expected from participants in FATF meetings and work projects.

results.title: Jamaica and Nigeria to join FATF Guest Initiative
results.description: Jamaica and Nigeria are the two new countries invited by the FATF President to participate in the FATF-Style Regional Body (FSRB) jurisdictions Guest Initiative aimed at fostering inclusiveness, enhancing the cohesion of the Global Network and strengthening global capacity in the fight against illicit finance.

results.title: Sint Maarten's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: The Caribbean Financial Action Task Force (CFATF) assessment of Sint Maarten's  measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing.

results.title: FATF to host webinars on Terrorist Financing Risks and Proliferation Financing and Sanctions Evasion Schemes
results.description: The FATF will hold two webinars this month to help strengthen global understanding across the public and private sectors of risks and vulnerabilities connected to terrorist financing and proliferation financing, aiming to improve responses to these evolving threats.

results.title: FATF Launches New Procedure to Address Unintended Consequences Affecting Non-Profit Organisations
results.description: The FATF has introduced a new process to address unintended consequences arising from the implementation of its Standards on non-profit organisations (NPOs), strengthening global efforts to protect legitimate NPO activity from disproportionate disruption.

results.title: Consolidated Processes and Procedures for Mutual Evaluations and Follow-Up Universal Procedures
results.description: The set of core elements that apply to all anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing assessments, in accordance with the FATF 2013 Methodology. These procedures were last revised in June 2025.

results.title: FATF report highlights evolving terrorist financing risks and warns of gaps in global understanding of the threats
results.description: The report, Comprehensive Update on Terrorist Financing Risks, reveals terrorists’ persistent ability to exploit the international financial system to support their activities and carry out attacks. With the TF methods varying depending on several factors and contexts, the report highlights terrorists’ adaptability, underscoring the need for risk-based counter-terrorist financing measures.

results.title: Algeria's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: Algeria has made progress in resolving some of the technical compliance shortcomings identified in its 2023 Mutual Evaluation Report.

results.title: FATF urges stronger global action to address Illicit Finance Risks in Virtual Assets
results.description: In its sixth targeted update on the global implementation of anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing (AML/CFT) measures to virtual assets (VA) and virtual asset service providers (VASPs), FATF highlights where stronger action is needed to safeguard the integrity of the international financial system.

results.title: FATF publishes new Guidance on Financial Inclusion and Anti-Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing Measures
results.description: This new FATF guidance highlights that financial inclusion and the fight against financial crime are mutually supportive. Enhanced financial sector transparency and integrity increases the reach and effectiveness of AML/CFT/CPF measures that help keep criminals out of the financial system and facilitate law enforcement investigations.

results.title: FATF Report highlights major gaps in global response to Proliferation Financing and Sanctions Evasion
results.description: This FATF report reveals that significant vulnerabilities remain across the global financial system in countering the financing of weapons of mass destruction (WMD).

results.title: Supporting the Global Network to strengthen the fight against illicit finance
results.description: The Global Network, comprising the FATF and the 9 FSRBs, is an inclusive framework that brings together more than 200 jurisdictions. They are united by a shared commitment to tackle illicit finance, to apply a single set of standards and a universal system of mutual evaluations.

results.title: Strengthening efforts to combat terrorist financing
results.description: The FATF has been working for 10 years to help countries stay ahead of terrorist financing risk. To improve this picture, the FATF will soon release a comprehensive analysis of terrorist financing, compiling cases provided by our Global Network.

results.title: Jurisdictions under Increased Monitoring - 13 June 2025
results.description:
Jurisdictions under increased monitoring are actively working with the FATF to address strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing. 
Croatia, Mali and  the Republic of Tanzania, are no longer subject to increased monitoring by the FATF.
Additional countries,  Bolivia and the Virgin Islands (UK), are now also subject to increased monitoring.

results.title: High-Risk Jurisdictions subject to a Call for Action - 13 June 2025
results.description: High-risk jurisdictions have significant strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and financing of proliferation. For all countries identified as high-risk, the FATF calls on all members and urges all jurisdictions to apply enhanced due diligence, and, in the most serious cases, countries are called upon to apply countermeasures to protect the international financial system from the money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing (ML/TF/PF) risks emanating from the country.

results.title: Outcomes Joint FATF-MONEYVAL Plenary, 12-13 June 2025
results.description: A joint FATF-MONEYVAL Plenary meeting hosted at the Council of Europe and chaired by FATF President, Elisa de Anda Madrazo, and MONEYVAL Chair, Nicola Muccioli, concluded today, strengthening global efforts to disrupt criminal activity.

results.title: FATF and MONEYVAL to partner on joint June 2025 Plenary meeting
results.description: The joint Plenary, chaired by FATF President, Elisa de Anda Madrazo, and MONEYVAL Chair, Nicola Muccioli, will follow two days of working group meetings. It will bring together members of the Global Network of more than 200 countries and international organisations, to reinforce the global response to the latest illicit finance threats.

results.title: Indonesia's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: Since the 2023 assessment of Indonesia's measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing, the country has taken a number of actions to strengthen its framework.

results.title: Forum on Cross-Border Payments Data holds first meeting
results.description: The first Forum on Cross-Border Payments Data was held on 20-21 May 2025 in Basel, bringing together experts in payments, anti-money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism (AML/CFT), sanctions and data privacy and protection.

results.title: Critical measures needed to fight money laundering and terrorist financing, say leaders of FATF, INTERPOL and UNODC
results.description: Leaders stress the strengthening of the FATF’s international standards on anti-money laundering and terrorism financing, and call for accelerated progress on co-operating across borders and capacity building ahead of the UN 2026 Crime Congress, to be hosted by the United Arab Emirates.

results.title: Senegal's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing

results.title: Private Sector Collaborative Forum March 2025
results.description: The FATF held its Private Sector Collaborative Forum (PSCF) on 25-27 March 2025 in Mumbai, India, hosted by the Reserve Bank of India and the Ministry of Finance. The PSCF is a unique platform for the FATF and its members to engage directly with the private sector and civil society, and deepen partnerships in the fight against money laundering and terrorist financing.

results.title: Public Consultation on Complex Proliferation Financing and Sanctions Evasion Schemes project
results.description: The FATF is undertaking a study aimed at improving country and private sector understanding of current Proliferation Financing (PF) risks. The resulting report will focus on providing a comprehensive up-to-date understanding of typologies in complex sanctions evasion schemes relevant to PF and identifying enforcement challenges and best practices, which helps to inform countries’ PF risk assessment and risk mitigation.

results.title: Public Consultation on AML/CFT and Financial Inclusion - Updated FATF Guidance on AML/CFT Measures and Financial Inclusion
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) is considering proposals for the update of the FATF Guidance on AML/CFT measures and financial inclusion. This is part of FATF’s programme of work to address the unintended consequences of AML/CFT measures.

results.title: FATF updates Standards and consults on guidance to better promote financial inclusion
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) has today updated its Standards after the February 2025 FATF Plenary approved changes to Recommendation 1 and its Interpretive Note, with corresponding amendments to Interpretive Notes to Recommendations 10 and 15, as well as related Glossary definitions to better support financial inclusion.

results.title: Second Public Consultation on Recommendation 16 on Payment Transparency
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) is holding a second round of public consultation on revisions to Recommendation 16 (R.16), its Interpretive Note (INR.16) and the related Glossary of specific terms, to adapt them to the changes in payment business models and messaging standards.

results.title: Money Laundering National Risk Assessment Guidance
results.description: To establish a strong national AML (Anti-Money Laundering) framework, countries must prioritise a deep understanding of money laundering risks. This guidance document supports countries in conducting a money laundering NRA, drawing on insights from over 90 countries within the FATF Global Network.

results.title: Côte d'Ivoire's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing

results.title: Public Consultation on AML/CFT and Financial Inclusion – proposed changes to FATF Standards
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) is considering revisions to the FATF Recommendations in order to better align them with measures to promote financial inclusion. This is part of FATF’s programme of work to address the unintended consequences of AML/CFT measures. FATF is inviting views and comments on the proposed changes from interested stakeholders.

results.title: High-Risk Jurisdictions subject to a Call for Action - 25 October 2024
results.description: High-risk jurisdictions have significant strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and financing of proliferation. For all countries identified as high-risk, the FATF calls on all members and urges all jurisdictions to apply enhanced due diligence, and, in the most serious cases, countries are called upon to apply countermeasures to protect the international financial system from the money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing (ML/TF/PF) risks emanating from the country.

results.title: Jurisdictions under Increased Monitoring - 25 October 2024
results.description:
Jurisdictions under increased monitoring are actively working with the FATF to address strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing. 
Additional countries, Algeria, Angola, Côte d’Ivoire and Lebanon, are now also subject to increased monitoring. 
Senegal is no longer subject to increased monitoring by the FATF.

results.title: Outcomes FATF Plenary, 23-25 October 2024
results.description: The first Financial Action Task Force (FATF) Plenary under the two-year Mexico Presidency of Elisa de Anda Madrazo concluded today. Delegates discussed key issues including the promotion of financial inclusion and the risk-based approach, a key priority of the FATF under its Mexican Presidency.

results.title: FATF Week – October 2024

results.title: FATF changes its grey listing criteria to further focus on risk
results.description: The FATF has made major changes to the criteria for putting countries on its lists to relieve pressures on least developed countries and focus on those countries posing greater risks to the international financial system.

results.title: Virtual Assets: Targeted Update on Implementation of the FATF Standards on VAs and VASPs
results.description: This report provides the fifth update on jurisdictions’ compliance with FATF’s Recommendation 15 and its Interpretative Note (R.15/INR.15). Recommendation 15 was updated in 2019 to apply anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing (AML/CFT) measures to virtual assets (VA) and virtual asset service providers (VASPs).

results.title: Horizontal Review of Gatekeepers’ Technical Compliance Related to Corruption
results.description: Corruption and money laundering are inextricably linked. Corrupt actors must launder bribes and misappropriated funds to enjoy their criminal profits. Through their role as gatekeepers to the financial system, non-financial professionals can facilitate, unwittingly or wittingly, high-level corruption.

results.title: Public consultation on FATF Money Laundering National Risk Assessment Guidance Update
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) is in the process of updating its guidance on National Risk Assessments (NRA), in particular concerning Money Laundering NRAs.

results.title: Urgent action needed to fight money laundering and terrorist financing, say Heads of FATF, INTERPOL and UNODC
results.description: While the Heads of FATF, INTERPOL and UNODC highlighted recent enhancements to FATF’s international standards on anti-money laundering and terrorism financing, they also called for accelerated progress on policy reforms and capacity building ahead of the UN 2026 Crime Congress, to be hosted by the United Arab Emirates.

results.title: FATF Ministers commit to stepping up efforts to fight money laundering, terrorist and proliferation financing
results.description: In their Ministerial Declaration, the Ministers of the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) reaffirmed their unwavering commitment to combat financial crime, and fully support the FATF as the global standard-setter for preventing and combatting money laundering, terrorist financing and the financing of the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction (AML/CFT/CPF).  FATF Ministers noted that despite significant progress made, there continue to be gaps in effective implementation of the FATF Standards and committed to swiftly implement measures to improve the effectiveness of outcomes in tackling ML/TF and PF.

results.title: Status of implementation of Recommendation 15 by  FATF Members and Jurisdictions with Materially Important VASP Activity
results.description: This table shows the steps FATF members and FSRB jurisdictions with materially important virtually asset service providers (VASP) activity have taken to implement the FATF Standards in relation to regulating and supervising VASPs.

results.title: Australia's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This is Australia's 4th Follow-Up report. It analyses Australia’s progress in addressing some of the technical compliance deficiencies identified in its Mutual Evaluation. Re-ratings are given where progress has been made.

results.title: Guidance on Beneficial Ownership and Transparency of Legal Arrangements
results.description:
In March 2023, the FATF strengthened its R.24 and R.25 on beneficial ownership with the publication of updated Guidance to assist the implementation of the R.24 requirements.  
The FATF Standards on transparency and beneficial ownership of legal arrangements – R.25 – require countries to assess the ML/TF risks linked to legal arrangements and take mitigating measures.

results.title: Russian Federation's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This report is an analysis of the results achieved by the Russian Federation between October 2019 and July 2023 in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies noted in their mutual evaluation.

results.title: Public Consultation on Recommendation 16 on Payment Transparency
results.description: The FATF is seeking views on proposed amendments to Recommendation 16, to reflect the rapid development of cross-border payment systems, and changes to industry standards, in particular ISO20022.  These revisions aim to help make cross-border payments faster, cheaper, more transparent and more inclusive whilst ensuring AML/CFT compliance, and ensure that FATF Recommendation 16 remains technology-neutral.  The public consultation is now closed.

results.title: Misuse of Citizenship and Residency by Investment Programmes
results.description: This  joint FATF-OECD report highlights how citizenship and residency by investment (CBI/RBI) programmes can allow criminals more global mobility and help them hide their identity and criminal activities behind shell companies in other jurisdictions.

results.title: Best Practices on Combating the Abuse of Non-Profit Organisations
results.description: The FATF has updated its Best Practices to reflect the amendments to Recommendation 8 and to help countries, the non-profit sector and financial institutions understand how best to protect relevant NPOs from abuse for terrorist financing, without unduly disrupting or discouraging legitimate NPO activities.

results.title: Recovering International Proceeds of Crime through Inter-Agency Networks
results.description: This report is a detailed study of the role and impact of Asset Recovery InterAgency Networks (ARINs). FATF’s analysis highlights successes of these informal international or regional networks, as well as the challenges that some of them face, such as resource constraints, lack of long-term financial sustainability.

results.title: Protecting non-profits from abuse for terrorist financing through the risk-based implementation of revised FATF Recommendation 8
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force today released amendments to Recommendation 8 and its Interpretive Note to address the misapplication and misinterpretation of Recommendation 8, that had led countries to apply disproportionate measures on Non-Profit Organisations (NPOs).

results.title: Amendments to the FATF Standards to Strengthen Global Asset Recovery
results.description: FATF released amendments to the FATF Recommendations which will provide law enforcement, financial intelligence units, prosecutors, other asset recovery practitioners and competent authorities with a more robust toolkit to target and confiscate criminal assets.

results.title: Myanmar's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: In light of the progress made by Myanmar since its MER was adopted, the country has been re-rated on one Recommendation.

results.title: Thailand's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: Overall, Thailand has made some progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in its MER and has been re-rated on two Recommendations.

results.title: Illicit Financial Flows from Cyber-enabled Fraud
results.description: Cyber-enabled fraud is a major transnational organised crime that has grown exponentially in recent years, both in volume of frauds reported and their global spread. Such crimes can have a devastating impact on individuals, organisations, and economies worldwide, causing significant financial losses and eroding trust in digital systems. The transnational nature of this crime, with proceeds of cyber-enabled fraud often rapidly transferred to different jurisdictions, makes this a global concern.

results.title: Cambodia's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: As a result of  progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in its MER, Cambodia has been re-rated on one Recommendation.

results.title: Mongolia's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: In light of the progress made by Mongolia since its MER was adopted, the country has been re-rated on one Recommendation.

results.title: Public Consultation - FATF Risk-Based Guidance on Recommendation 25
results.description: The FATF is developing updated risk-based guidance on Recommendation 25 on Beneficial Ownership and Transparency of Legal Arrangements. The FATF is seeking comments from interested stakeholders, including companies and other legal persons, financial institutions, designated non-financial businesses and professions, and non-profit organisations before finalising the guidance. The updated guidance reflects the February 2023 revisions to Recommendation 25 and complements the existing guidance on Recommendation 24 on legal persons. The guidance aims to help stakeholders from the public and private sectors that are involved in trusts or similar legal arrangements to assess and mitigate money laundering and terrorist financing risks.

results.title: Crowdfunding for Terrorism Financing
results.description: This report analyses how terrorists have exploited fundraising platforms and crowdfunding activities on social media to seek funding for their terrorist cause from a global audience. It highlights challenges, good practices and risk indicators to help public and private sector entities identify potential attempts at terrorist financing using crowdfunding.

results.title: FATF-INTERPOL Partnership: Igniting Global Change to Take the Profit Out of Crime
results.description: At the second FATF-INTERPOL Roundtable Engagement (FIRE) over 200 experts from around the globe are discussing challenges, good practices and recent developments to more effectively go after criminals’ ill-gotten gains.  Participants discussed the progress in formal and informal co-operation and welcomed FATF’s proposals to strengthen asset recovery networks.

results.title: Public Consultation on the FATF Best Practice Paper to Combat the Abuse of Non Profit Organisations
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) is considering proposals for the update of the FATF Best Practice Paper to Combat the Abuse of NPOs. This project is being undertaken in parallel to the proposed amendments to Recommendation 8 and its Interpretive Note (also under public consultation) to better clarify the implementation of a risk-based approach.

results.title: Public Consultation on the Revision of Recommendation 8 and its Interpretive Note
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) is considering proposals for draft amendment text to Recommendation 8 of the FATF Standards and its Interpretive Note (R.8/INR.8), which aims to protect NPOs from potential terrorist financing abuse.

results.title: Virtual Assets: Targeted Update on Implementation of the FATF Standards on VAs and VASPs
results.description: This report provides an update on implementation of the FATF's anti-money laundering and counter terrorist financing standards on virtual assets and virtual assets service providers.  This is the fourth update  since the FATF extended its Standards to this sector. The report finds that global implementation of these strengthened measures remains relatively poor.  The lack of regulation creates significant loopholes for criminals to exploit. Closing the gaps in global regulation of virtual assets is an urgent priority. The FATF calls on all countries to apply the AML/CFT rules to virtual assets service providers, without further delay.

results.title: High-Risk Jurisdictions subject to a Call for Action  - June 2023
results.description: High-risk jurisdictions have significant strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and financing of proliferation. For all countries identified as high-risk, the FATF calls on all members and urges all jurisdictions to apply enhanced due diligence, and, in the most serious cases, countries are called upon to apply counter-measures to protect the international financial system from the money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing (ML/TF/PF) risks emanating from the country..

results.title: Jurisdictions under Increased Monitoring - 23 June 2023
results.description: Jurisdictions under increased monitoring are actively working with the FATF to address strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing. New jurisdictions subject to increased monitoring are Cameroon, Croatia and Vietnam.

results.description: The third Financial Action Task Force (FATF) Plenary under the two-year Singapore Presidency of T. Raja Kumar will take place on 21-23 June and will conclude FATF Week which starts on 19 June.

results.title: Mexico's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing

results.title: Madagascar's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: Madagascar has made significant overall progress in resolving the technical compliance shortcomings identified in its 2018 Mutual Evaluation Report.

results.title: Qatar's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: Qatar has made substantive improvements to its system to combat money laundering and terrorism financing and its technical compliance with FATF requirements is very strong. However, Qatar needs to make some major improvements, in its criminal justice response to terrorist financing.

results.title: Fiji's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This report identifies the progress Fiji has made in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in its 2016 Mutual Evaluation.

results.title: Private Sector Consultative Forum May 2023
results.description: The FATF held its annual Private Sector Consultative Forum (PSCF) on 8th and 9th May 2023 in Vienna, Austria, hosted by the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC). The PSCF is a unique platform for the FATF and its members to engage directly with the private sector and civil society, and deepen partnerships in the fight against money laundering and terrorist financing.

results.title: Indonesia's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: Indonesia has a strong legal framework to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing and is making good use of financial intelligence and domestic and international cooperation. Indonesia needs to focus more on improving asset recovery, risk-based supervision and proportionate and dissuasive sanctions.

results.title: Press Release - Virtual Assets Contact Group (VACG)
results.description: This week, the Virtual Assets Contact Group of the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) met in Tokyo to discuss global progress and challenges in regulating virtual assets. The Group stressed that countries worldwide need to urgently implement the FATF Standards to effectively regulate and supervise this sector.

results.title: FATF Joint Experts’ Meeting and FATF/UNODC Joint Workshop on Hawala 3-6 April 2023
results.description: During the three-day FATF Joint Experts’ Meeting and FATF/UNODC Joint Workshop on Hawala, hosted by the Indian Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue, experts exchanged insights on money laundering and terrorist financing risks including cyber-enabled fraud, the misuse of citizenship and residency investment programs and terrorist financing through crowdfunding.

results.title: Venezuela's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: The Mutual Evaluation Report presents a comprehensive review of the effectiveness of the country's AML/CFT system and its level of compliance with the FATF 40 Recommendations as at the time of the on-site visit from 17 to 28 January 2022.

results.title: Countering Ransomware Financing
results.description: This FATF report analyses the methods that criminals use to carry out their ransomware attacks and how payments are made and laundered. The report also proposes a number of actions that countries can take to more effectively disrupt ransomware-related money laundering.  This report is complemented by Countering Ransomware financing: potential risk indicators which will help public and private sector entities identify suspicious activities related to ransomware.

results.title: Guidance on Beneficial Ownership of Legal Persons
results.description: In March 2022, the FATF agreed on tougher global beneficial ownership standards in its Recommendation 24 by requiring countries to ensure that competent authorities have access to adequate, accurate and up-to-date information on the true owners of companies. The FATF has now updated the guidance that will help countries implement the revised Recommendation 24.

results.title: Learning and Development Forum on Asset Targeting and Recovery
results.description: Over 300 judicial, law enforcement, FIU, tax and other operational experts, as well as policy makers from around the globe participated in this first Learning and Development Forum, hosted by Guardia di Finanza of Italy, to learn and share experiences to improve and make a difference in the fight against financial crime.

results.title: Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing in the Art and Antiquities Market
results.description: Criminals, organized crime groups and terrorists have abused the market of art and antiquities to launder money and fund their activities. This report examines the link between money laundering and terrorist financing and the market of art and antiquities. It also highlights best practices and risk indicators that could help to improve global responses to such threats.

For all countries identified as high-risk, the FATF calls on all members and urges all jurisdictions to apply enhanced due diligence, and, in the most serious cases, countries are called upon to apply counter-measures to protect the international financial system from the money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing (ML/TF/PF risks emanating from the country.

results.title: Jurisdictions under Increased Monitoring - 24 February 2023
results.description: Jurisdictions under increased monitoring are actively working with the FATF to address strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing. New countries subject to increased monitoring are South Africa and Nigeria. Morocco and Cambodia are no longer subject to increased monitoring by the FATF.

results.title: FATF Statement on the Russian Federation
results.description: The Russian Federation’s continuing and intensifying war of aggression against Ukraine runs counter to FATF’s principles of promoting security, safety and the integrity of the global financial system and the commitment to international cooperation and mutual respect upon which FATF Members have agreed to implement and support the FATF Standards. As a result, the FATF Plenary has today suspended the membership of the Russian Federation.

results.title: Risk-Based Approach Guidance for the Real Estate Sector - summary of the FATF public consultation
results.description: The FATF held a public consultation on its draft Guidance for the real estate sector from 7 March until 22 April 2022.

results.title: Risk-based Approach Guidance for the Real Estate Sector
results.description: Real estate is a popular choice for investment, but it also attracts criminals who use real estate to assist in their illicit activities or to launder their criminal profits.  The real estate sector needs to effectively implement measures to detect and prevent money laundering and terrorist financing. This guidance provides real estate professionals, supervisors, regulators and policy-makers with strategies and tools towards a more effective anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing system.

results.title: Aruba's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This report provides a summary of the anti-money laundering and combating financing of terrorism (AML/CFT) measures in place in Aruba as at the date of the on-site visit (30 August to 10 September 2021). It analyses the level of compliance with the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) 40 Recommendations and the level of effectiveness of Aruba's AML/CFT system and provides recommendations on how the system could be strengthened.

results.title: Grenada's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing

It analyses the level of compliance with the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) 40 Recommendations and the level of effectiveness of Grenada's AML/CFT system and provides recommendations on how the system could be strengthened.

results.title: Partnering in the Fight Against Financial Crime: Data Protection, Technology and Private Sector Information Sharing
results.description: This report aims to help jurisdictions responsibly enhance, design and implement information collaboration initiatives among private sector entities, in accordance with data protection and privacy DPP rules.

results.title: FATF President at the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors' Meeting, 15-16 July 2022
results.description: FATF President T. Raja Kumar's remarks at his first G20 FMCBG Meeting since assuming the Presidency of the FATF.

results.title: Webinar on Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing Risks arising from Migrant Smuggling
results.description: In this webinar panelists discussed how to better detect smugglers’ illegal money and what state authorities, private sector and NPOs can do in order to fight this illegal business and ensure legal ways of migration for those who seek better opportunities in life.

results.title: Bulgaria's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing

results.title: Objectives for the FATF during the Singapore Presidency 2022-2024
results.description: An overview of the objectives during the Singapore Presidency period July 2022-June 2024 as presented by FATF President T. Raja Kumar to the June 2022 Plenary.

results.title: Targeted Update on Implementation of FATF’s Standards on VAs and VASPs
results.description: FATF has produced a targeted update on implementation of its Standards on virtual assets and virtual asset service providers, with a focus on FATF’s Travel Rule. This report comes three years after FATF extended its anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing (AML/CFT measures to VAs and VASPs to prevent criminal and terrorist misuse of the sector.

results.title: Revision of Recommendation 25 - White Paper for Public Consultation
results.description: The Financial Action Task Force FATF is conducting a review of Recommendation 25 R.25 on the transparency and beneficial ownership BO of legal arrangements.

results.title: Jurisdictions under Increased Monitoring - June 2022
results.description: Jurisdictions under increased monitoring are actively working with the FATF to address strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing.

results.title: Outcomes FATF Plenary, 14-17 June 2022
results.description: The German government hosted this last Plenary under the two-year German Presidency of Dr Marcus Pleyer, in Berlin. Delegates discussed key issues including a report to prevent money laundering through the real estate sector and a report to help assess and mitigate the money laundering and terrorist financing risks

results.title: FATF Statement on the Russian Federation
results.description: The FATF again expresses its deepest sympathies for the people of Ukraine and continues to deplore the huge loss of life and needless destruction caused by the ongoing Russian invasion. The Russian actions run counter to the FATF core principles aiming to promote security, safety and the integrity of the global financial system. They also represent a gross violation of the commitment to international cooperation and mutual respect upon which FATF Members have agreed to implement and support the FATF Standards.

results.title: High-Risk Jurisdictions subject to a Call for Action - June 2022
results.description: High-risk jurisdictions have significant strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and financing of proliferation. For all countries identified as high-risk, the FATF calls on all members and urges all jurisdictions to apply enhanced due diligence, and, in the most serious cases, countries are called upon to apply counter-measures to protect the international financial system from the money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing (ML/TF/PF risks emanating from the country.

results.title: FATF Conference on Digital Transformation
results.description: FATF Ministers have identified Digital Transformation as a strategic priority of the FATF. More than 100 experts in financial crime, data protection, and technology gathered at a conference to discuss the potential benefits and challenges of digital transformation in the fight against money laundering and terrorist financing.

results.title: FATF Plenary, June 2022
results.description: During four days of meetings, delegates will finalise key issues including a report to prevent money laundering through the real estate sector and a report that will help financial institutions assess and mitigate the money laundering and terrorist financing risks they face.

results.title: AML/CFT Digital Strategy for Law Enforcement Authorities
results.description: The FATF completed work that explores how law enforcement agencies can use technology to successfully investigate money laundering and terrorist financing, mitigate the risks of these crimes, and share information in a secure manner.

results.description: On 1 June 2022, Violaine Clerc assumed the role of FATF Executive Secretary of the Financial Action Task Force FATF.

results.title: The future of the Global Network - Strategic Vision
results.description: The Ministers of the Financial Action Task Force FATF members committed to take swift and decisive action to improve the effectiveness of measures to fight money laundering, terrorist and proliferation financing at their meeting in Washington D.C. today.

results.title: FATF Ministers commit to take decisive action against money laundering, terrorist and proliferation financing
results.description: Ministers and other top officials from 37 FATF member countries and two regional organisations agreed to strengthen the FATF Global Network, support the FATF system of mutual evaluations, ensure sustainable funding for the FATF to implement its strategic priorities, and leverage digital transformation to better tackle money laundering and terrorist financing.

results.title: FATF Ministerial Meeting
results.description: On 21 April, Ministers of the Financial Action Task Force will meet to discuss the role of the FATF in taking action against the crimes that impact our society and threaten global security.

results.title: Report on the State of Effectiveness and Compliance with the FATF Standards
results.description: This landmark report gives a comprehensive overview of the state of global efforts to tackle money laundering, terrorist and proliferation financing. It is based on data from FATF and FSRB mutual evaluation reports since 2013, which assessed the strengths and weaknesses of national frameworks to tackle these crimes.

results.title: Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing Risks Arising from Migrant Smuggling
results.description: This report identifies the most common methods to transfer and launder the proceeds of migrant smuggling and provides several recommendations and good practices to better identify and investigate these criminal proceeds.

results.title: Public Statement on revisions to R.24
results.description: The revisions to Recommendation 24 will require countries to follow a risk-based approach and consider the risks of legal persons in their countries. results.description: This review looks at how jurisdictions and the private sector have implemented the revised Standards since the FATF’s first 12-month review. It also looks at changes in the typologies, risks and the market structure of the virtual assets sector.

results.title: Stocktake on Data Pooling, Collaborative Analytics and Data Protection
results.description: This report examines commercially available or emerging technologies that facilitate advanced AML/CFT analytics within regulated entities. It also looks at technologies that allow collaborative analytics between financial institutions, while respecting national and international data privacy and protection legal frameworks.

results.title: Opportunities and Challenges of New Technologies for AML/CFT
results.description: This FATF report aims to increase awareness of and identify opportunities to leverage emerging and existing technology-based solutions. It identifies the conditions, policies and practices that can help support the development and adoption of new technologies that contribute to the efficiency and effectiveness of AML/CFT.

results.title: Ethnically or Racially Motivated Terrorism Financing
results.description: This report aims to increase the understanding of terrorist financing risks related to extreme right wing actors. It encourages countries to continue to develop their understanding of this increasingly transnational criminal activity, including by considering ethnically or racially motivated terrorism financing in their national risk assessments.

results.title: Guidance on Proliferation Financing Risk Assessment and Mitigation
results.description: The FATF Guidance on Proliferation Financing Risk Assessment and Mitigation will help countries, financial institutions, designated non-financial businesses and professions DNFBPs and virtual asset service providers effectively implement FATFs requirements to identify, assess, understand and mitigate their proliferation financing risks.

results.title: Money Laundering from Environmental Crime
results.description: This report looks at the methods that criminals use to launder the proceeds of environmental crime. When properly implemented, the FATF Recommendations provide effective tools to go after these illicit financial flows.

results.title: Revisions to Recommendation 24 - White Paper for Public Consultation
results.description: The FATF is considering a number of potential changes to Recommendation 24, including the potential for updates in the areas set out below. FATF’s work in this area is ongoing, and will benefit from hearing views from stakeholders, including companies and other legal persons, financial institutions, designated non-financial businesses and professions DNFBPs, and non-profit organisations. This public consultation is now closed.

results.title: Jurisdictions under Increased Monitoring - June 2021
results.description: Jurisdictions under increased monitoring are actively working with the FATF to address strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing.

results.title: Outcomes FATF Plenary, 20-25 June 2021
results.description: During this 5-day virtal Plenary delegates from the Global Network and observer organisations discussed key issues to strengthen global action against the financial flows that fuel crime and terrorism. These included the mutual evaluation of South Africa and Japan.

results.title: High-Risk Jurisdictions subject to a Call for Action - June 2021
results.description: High-risk jurisdictions have significant strategic deficiencies in their regimes to counter money laundering, terrorist financing, and financing of proliferation. For all countries identified as high-risk, the FATF calls on all members and urges all jurisdictions to apply enhanced due diligence, and in the most serious cases, countries are called upon to apply counter-measures to protect the international financial system from the ongoing money laundering, terrorist financing, and proliferation financing risks emanating from the country.

results.title: Mexico's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This follow-up report analyses Mexico's progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in its 2018 Mutual Evaluation report.

results.title: Follow-up report to Bhutan's assessment of anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing measures
results.description: This follow-up report analyses the progress of Bhutan in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in its MER and FURs.

results.title: Follow-up report to Pakistan's assessment of anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing measures
results.description: This follow-up report analyses the progress of Pakistan in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in its MER and FURs.

results.title: Environmental Crime
results.description: FATF's work on money laundering from environmental crimes aims to raise awareness about the financial flows that fuel crimes such as illegal logging, illegal wildlife trade and waste trafficking.

results.title: Money laundering and terrorist financing risks
results.description: The effectiveness of measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing depend on a country's assessment and understanding of the risks it is exposed to, and the extent to which it has mitigated these risks. This page includes examples of  published national risk assessments NRAs and regional risk assessments.

results.title: The Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC)'s measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This report summarizes the anti-money laundering counter financing of terrorism (AML/CFT) measures in place in the Democratic Republic of Congo as at the date of the on-site visit from 13-24 August 2018. It analyses the level of compliance with the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) 40 Recommendations and the level of effectiveness of DRC's AML/CFT system and provides recommendations on how the system could be strengthened.

results.title: New Zealand's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: New Zealand’s measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing are delivering good results, but the country needs to focus more on improving the availability of beneficial ownership information, strengthening supervision and implementation of targeted financial sanctions.

results.title: Webinar on Risk-Based Supervision
results.description: FATF organised a webinar with key speakers from around the world, to discuss the challenges of effective supervision.

results.title: G20 confirm their support for the FATF as the global standard-setter to prevent money laundering, terrorist financing and proliferation financing.
results.description: The G20 supports the FATF as global standard-setter to prevent money laundering, terrorist financing and proliferation financing.

results.title: Joint meeting of the FATF and the G20 Anti-Corruption Working Group
results.description: FATF and G20 Anti-Corruption Working Group's joint meeting on improving beneficial ownership transparency.

results.title: Marcus Pleyer speaks at Second G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meeting, 7 April 2021
results.description: FATF President, Marcus Pleyer, commends the Italian G20 presidency's hard work to ensure economic recovery but warns against the dangers of illicit financial flows.

results.title: Public consultation on FATF draft guidance on a risk-based approach to virtual assets and virtual asset service providers
results.description: The FATF is updating its Guidance on the risk-based approach to virtual assets VAs and virtual asset service providers VASPs.
results.imagePath: /content/dam/fatf-gafi/public-consultation/Thumbnail March 2021 - VA Guidance update - Sixth draft - Public consultation.jpg

results.title: Webinar: Trade-Based Money Laundering
results.description: This webinar, aimed at public and private sector representatives, will discuss the most recent trade-based money laundering trends and developments.

results.title: High Level Roundtable for Data Pooling, Analysis and Data Protection, 10-11 March 2021
results.description: Summary of the high-level roundtable with AML/CFT supervisors and data protection authorities to discuss data pooling, analysis and data protection in the context of the fight against money laundering and terrorist financing.

results.title: Trade-Based Money Laundering: Risk Indicators
results.description: The trade-based money laundering: risk indicators supplement the trade-based money laundering: trends and developments report and will help public and private sector identify possible trade-based money laundering.

results.title: The Cayman Islands' progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This report analyses the Cayman Islands' progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in the assessment of their measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing of March 2019.

results.title: Guidance on Risk-Based Supervision
results.description: This Guidance on risk-based supervision will help supervisors address the full spectrum of risks and focus resources where the risks are highest. The risk-based approach will make supervisors' efforts to detect and prevent the financial flows that fuel crime and terrorism more effective.

results.title: Speech at the Chatham House Illicit Financial Flows Conference, 1-2 March 2021
results.description: FATF, as the global body responsible for AML, makes clear the link between preventive measures like customer due diligence, and the crimes those measures are intended to prevent and detect, such as people trafficking and the wildlife trade.

results.title: Public Consultation on FATF Guidance on Proliferation Financing Risk Assessment and Mitigation
results.description: FATF is developing Guidance to help both public and private sectors in implementing the new requirements defined in Recommendation 1 and its Interpretive Note. The Guidance seeks to assist both sectors in conducting a risk assessment in the context of proliferation financing, and applying corresponding risk mitigation measures. The public consultation is now closed.
results.imagePath: /content/dam/fatf-gafi/public-consultation/Thumbnail-Public Consultation Guidance Proliferation Financing Risk Assessment and Mitigation.jpg

results.title: Remarks at the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meeting, 26 February
results.description: FATF President Marcus Pleyer commends the G20 Italian Presidency's determination to make better use of digitalisation in financial services, and calls on the G20 to step up the fight against illicit financial flows by fully and effectively implementing FATF standards.

results.title: FATF/Egmont Trade-based Money Laundering: Trends and Developments
results.description: Using numerous case studies from around the FATF’s Global Network, this new FATF-Egmont Group report aims to help public and private sector with the challenges of detecting trade-based money laundering.

results.title: Opening remarks at Trade-Based Money Laundering Press Conference
results.description: Remarks by FATF President Marcus Pleyer at the press conference on the launch of the FATF-Egmont Group report Trade-Based Money Laundering.

results.title: Address by the FATF President to the GAFILAT Virtual Plenary Meeting
results.description: FATF President addresses the GAFILAT Virtual Plenary Meeting and congratulates GAFILAT on the occasion of their 20th Anniversary.

results.title: FATF President speaks at annual Israel Money Laundering and Terror Financing Prohibition Authority IMPA Conference
results.description: FATF President Marcus Pleyer, speaking at the annual IMPA conference, marks the 20th anniversary of the Israeli anti-money laundering law and the second anniversary of Israel's membership of the FATF.

results.title: Joint meeting of experts 23-26 November 2020
results.description: Experts from various governments around the world as well as international organisations, private sector and civil society shared views and insights during four days of meetings.  These events allow the FATF to better understand the challenges they are facing and to design the tools and mechanisms that will be most effective in the fight against money laundering and terrorist financing.

results.title: G20 Leaders support the FATF’s work to address money laundering and terrorist financing risks
results.description: G20 leaders participating in the Riyadh Summit, reiterated their support to the FATF as the global standard-setting body to prevent money laundering, terrorist financing and proliferation financing.  In his remarks to G20 Leaders, FATF President Marcus Pleyer said that these crimes undermine just competition, hamper growth, deepen inequality, and erode confidence in the integrity of the global financial system.

results.title: FATF President's remarks to G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting
results.description: The FATF Standards help governments to contain the economic vulnerabilities associated with financial crime. World leaders need to keep the fight against money laundering high on their agendas.

results.title: Iceland's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This follow-up report analyses Iceland’s progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in its 2018 Mutual Evaluation and ensuing 2019 Follow-Up Report.

results.title: Opening Remarks by FATF Executive Secretary at V20 Summit
results.description: The FATF will not tolerate jurisdictions leaving loopholes in VASP regulations that criminals can exploit. We assess countries against global standards and will not shy away from identifying those who expose financial systems to risks.

results.title: The Slovak Republic's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This report summarizes the anti-money laundering counter financing of terrorism (AML/CFT) measures in place in Slovak Republic as at the date of the on-site visit from 7-18 October 2019. It analyses the level of compliance with the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) 40 Recommendations and the level of effectiveness of Slovak Republic’s AML/CFT system and provides recommendations on how the system could be strengthened.

results.title: The importance of allocating sufficient resources to AML/CFT regimes during the COVID-19 pandemic
results.description: The FATF, its members and observers, the FATF-style regional bodies and members of the global network are continuing to work together to understand the impacts of the pandemic on money laundering, terrorist financing and on the operation of anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing (AML/CFT) systems

results.title: FATF President's remarks to G20 Anti-Corruption Ministerial
results.description: FATF President Marcus Pleyer demonstrates how by tackling money laundering, the global financial system tackles corruption. He also highlighted the FATF Report to the G20 on so-called stablecoins, which concludes that the FATF's binding standards for virtual assets and virtual asset service providers, also clearly apply to so-called stablecoins.

results.description: Money laundering fuels serious crime and terrorism, and undermines the integrity of the global financial system. results.title: United Arab Emirates' measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing

results.title: Best Practices on Beneficial Ownership for Legal Persons
results.description: Transparency of beneficial ownership is essential to prevent the misuse of companies, associations or other entities for money laundering or terrorist financing. However, many jurisdictions find it challenging to effectively implement the FATF’s Recommendations in this area. These FATF best practices suggests key features of an effective system, using input from countries around the global network. It will help countries implement robust and effective measures to prevent criminals and terrorists from hiding behind companies and other legal persons. The paper also suggests options for jurisdictions to obtain beneficial ownership information of overseas entities.

This report summarizes the AML/CFT measures in Burkina Faso at the date of the on-site visit. It analyses the level of compliance with the FATF 40 Recommendations and the level of effectiveness of Burkina Faso’s AML/CFT system, and recommends how the system could be strengthened.
This evaluation was based on the 2012 FATF Recommendations, and was prepared using the 2013 Methodology. The evaluation was based on information provided by Burkina Faso, and information obtained by the evaluation team during its on-site visit to Burkina Faso from 23 July to 3 August 2018 and was adopted by GIABA at its May 2019 Plenary meeting.

results.title: Financial Action Task Force - Thirty Years
results.description:
G7 leaders created the Financial Action Task Force at their Summit in 1989.  They mandated this new task force, the FATF, with examining money laundering techniques and trends, reviewing the action which had already been taken at a national or international level, and setting out the measures that needed to be taken to combat money laundering.  

This publication retraces the progress that has made during the three decades that followed, including the expansion of the FATF's mandate to include the fight against terrorist financing and the financing of weapons of mass destruction.  It also sets out the work that lies ahead for the members of the FATF and the FATF Global Network.

results.title: Morocco's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing

The report provides recommendations on how Morocco's AML/CFT system could be strengthened. The findings of this assessment have also been reviewed and endorsed by the FATF.

The assessment is a comprehensive review of the effectiveness of Zambia's AML/CFT system and its level of compliance with the FATF Recommendations.

results.title: Guidance for a Risk-Based Approach Guidance for Legal Professionals
results.description: The guidance aims to support legal professionals in the design of effective measure to manage their ML/TF risks, when establishing or maintaining business relationships. In particular, it explains the obligation for legal professionals to identify and verify beneficial ownership information and provides examples of simplified, standard and enhanced CDD measures.

results.title: FATF Guidance for a Risk-Based Approach for the Accounting Profession
results.description: This guidance highlights the need for a sound assessment of the money laundering and terrorist financing risks that accountants face so that the policies, procedures and ongoing customer due diligence measures mitigate these risks. The risk-based approach is central to the effective implementation of the FATF Recommendations to fight money laundering and terrorist financing.

results.title: FATF Guidance for a Risk-Based Approach for Trust and Company Service Providers
results.description: This guidance aims to support the implementation of the risk-based approach to trust and company service providers, taking into account national money laundering and terrorist financing risk assessments and AML/CFT legal and regulatory frameworks.

results.title: Public Statement on Virtual Assets and Related Providers
results.description: FATF is setting out more detailed implementation requirements for effective regulation and supervision/monitoring of virtual asset services providers, in order to adequately mitigate the ML/TF risks associated with virtual asset activities. At its Plenary of June 2019, the FATF finalised the text of the new Interpretive Note for Recommendation 15.

results.title: New UN resolution reaffirms the close collaboration between the FATF and the United Nations in the fight against terrorist fighting
results.description: No country is immune from terrorism. All countries are at risk of financing terrorism, whether or not they themselves have been the victims of terrorist attacks. The Security Council adopted a new resolution 2462, committing to further work with the FATF to prevent, detect and disrupt the financing of terrorism.

results.title: Guidance for a Risk-Based Approach: Life Insurance Sector
results.description:
The risk-based approach (RBA) is central to the effective implementation of the revised FATF International Standards on Combating Money Laundering and the Financing of Terrorism and Proliferation, which were adopted in 2012. The FATF has reviewed its 2009 RBA Guidance for the life insurance sector, in order to bring it in line with the revised FATF requirements.    
This updated RBA Guidance for the life insurance sector was drafted by a group of FATF members and observers, and representatives of the private sector, co-led by representatives of France and Manulife.

The report provides a summary of the anti-money laundering (AML) and countering the financing of terrorism (CFT) measures in place in the Principality of Andorra (Andorra) as at the date of the on-site visit (between 6 and 18 March 2017). It analyses the level of compliance with the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) 40 Recommendations and the level of effectiveness of Andorra’s AML/CFT system, and provides recommendations on how the system could be strengthened.

results.title: Consolidated FATF Standards on Information Sharing
results.description: This document groups the relevant sections on information sharing from the FATF Recommendations, to clarify the types of information that should be shared, when it should be shared and the safeguards and protections that should apply to sharing information.

results.title: CFATF Public Statement on Haiti
results.description: The Caribbean Financial Action Task Force (CFATF) issued a public statement on Haiti's progress to address strategic deficiencies in its anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing (AML/CFT regime. Haiti is no longer subject to the CFATF-ICRG review process.

results.title: CFATF XLVI Plenary Meeting, Georgetown, Guyana, 15 November
results.description: FATF Executive Secretary David Lewis commends CFATF on its commitment and contribution to the Global Network and encourages CFATF to continue its own efforts to further improve the quality of mutual evaluations.

results.title: Nicaragua's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This GAFILAT report summarises the measures of Anti-Money Laundering and Combating Financing of Terrorism (AML/CFT) existing in the Republic of Nicaragua as of the on-site visit from 9th to 20th January 2017. It analyses the compliance level with the FATF 40 Recommendations, as well as the effectiveness level of the Nicaraguan AML/CFT system, and it offers recommendations on how the system can be strengthened.

results.title: Improving Global AML/CFT Compliance: On-going Process - 3 November 2017
results.description:
The FATF updated its statements identifying jurisdictions which have strategic AML/CFT deficiencies for which they have developed an action plan with the FATF. 

The FATF updated its statements identifying jurisdictions which have strategic AML/CFT deficiencies for which they have developed an action plan with the FATF. Uganda is no longer subject to the FATF's On-Going Global AML/CFT Compliance Process.

results.title: Outcomes Joint FATF/GAFILAT Plenary, 1-3 November 2017
results.description: Delegates discussed counter-terrorist financing, financial inclusion, information sharing and measures to combat money laundering and the financing of terrorism and proliferation in Portugal and Mexico. They also discussed the follow-up report for Austria, issued a statement on Brazil, discussed AML/CFT improvements in Uganda and issued a statement on DPRK.

results.title: FATF Guidance - Private Sector Information Sharing
results.description: This FATF guidance clarifies key FATF Standards on information sharing and provides examples to overcome information sharing, both group-wide withing financial groups, and between financial institutions which are not part of the same group.

results.title: FATF Guidance on AML/CFT measures and financial inclusion, with a supplement on customer due diligence
results.description: This FATF Guidance aims to provide support in designing Anti-Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing (AML/CFT) measures that meet the goal of financial inclusion, without compromising the measures that exist for the purpose of combating crime. The revised Guidance, reflects the changes made to the FATF Recommendations in 2012 and focuses in particular on the reinforcement of the risk-based approach (RBA), as a general and underlying principle of all AML/CFT systems.

results.title: FATF Statement on the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK)
results.description: In order to address the threat posed by the DPRK’s illicit activities related to the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction WMD and its financing, the FATF calls upon its members and urges all jurisdictions to effectively implement the relevant FATF recommendations and UNSC Resolutions.

results.title: The FATF position on FinTech and RegTech
results.description: FATF statement that emphasises that FATF strongly supports responsible financial innovation that is in line with the AML/CFT requirements found in the FATF Standards, and will continue to explore the opportunities that new financial and regulatory technologies may present for improving the effective implementation of AML/CFT measures.

results.title: Public Statement
results.description: In its public statement identifying jurisdictions with strategic deficiencies in anti-money laundering / countering the financing of terrorism measures, the FATF continues to calls on countries to apply counter-measures to the Democratic People's Republic of Korea.

results.title: Opening Speech by the President of Argentina, Mauricio Macri, at the Joint FATF/GAFILAT Plenary, Buenos Aires, 1-3 November 2017
results.description: Mr. Mauricio Macri, President of the Republic of Argentina, opened the FATF-GAFILAT Joint Plenary meeting in Buenos Aires on November 1, 2017.

results.title: Technical Analysis of FATF Recommendations: Rerating of Costa Rica - 2017
results.description: This report analyses Costa Rica's progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in the GAFILAT Mutual Evaluation Report of 2015 and its 2016 follow-up report.

results.title: Samoa's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This report analyses Samoa's progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in the APG Mutual Evaluation Report of 2015.

results.title: Technical Analysis of FATF Recommendations: Re-rating of Cuba - 2017
results.description: This report analyses Cuba' progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in the GAFILAT Mutual Evaluation Report of 2015.

results.title: Fiji's progress in strengthening measures to tackle money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This report analyses Fiji's progress in addressing the technical compliance deficiencies identified in the APG 3rd Round Mutual Evaluation Report of 2016.

results.title: US-MENA Private Sector Dialogue (PSD)
results.description: Speech by David Lewis on “Combating Terrorism and Enhancing Relations with Correspondent Banks” at the US-MENA Private Sector Dialogue PSD that took place in Washington on October 16, 2017.

results.title: Botswana's measures to combat money laundering and terrorist financing
results.description: This report provides a summary of the AML/CFT measures in place in Botswana as at the date of the on-site visit 13-24 June 2016.

results.title: FATF FinTech and RegTech Forum, Berlin
results.description: Outcomes from the FATF FinTech and RegTech Forum which took place on 9-10 October 2017 in Berlin, Germany.
